deep_dive.title: ITNet: A Learnable Integral Transform That Subsumes Convolution, Attention, and Recurrence
deep_dive.abstract: Convolutional networks, recurrent networks, and transformers each encode different inductive biases -- locality, sequential memory, and content-dependent pairwise interaction -- and have remained mathematically distinct since their inception. We show that this fragmentation reflects not a fundamental diversity in how signals should be processed, but rather incomplete views of a single underlying mathematical object: a learnable integral transform. We introduce the Integral Transform Network (ITNet), a unified architecture built around a learnable kernel that depends jointly on positions and features. This kernel is implemented as a small neural network, specifically an MLP, that models pairwise interactions, enabling the model to adapt its behavior from data. We show that convolution, self-attention (including multi-head), and autoregressive recurrence (including LSTM, GRU, S4, and Mamba) arise as special cases under appropriate parameterizations, and that ITNet is a universal approximator of continuous operators. To make this practical, we develop tiled kernel fusion, importance-weighted Monte Carlo integration, and learned low-rank factorization, enabling efficient and scalable computation. A single ITNet architecture with a shared operator and lightweight modality-specific encoders matches or exceeds specialized baselines on ImageNet-1K , GLUE, ModelNet40, VQA\,v2 and NLVR2. The results demonstrate that a single learned interaction mechanism can recover the behavior of all three architectural families from data.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: novel unified architecture subsuming convolution, attention, recurrence
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 新颖的统一架构，融合卷积、注意力与循环
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 新颖的统一架构，融合卷积、注意力与循环
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 新颖的统一架构，融合卷积、注意力与循环
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: ITNet proposes a learnable integral transform that unifies convolution, attention, and recurrence into a single novel architecture, offering a new design paradigm potentially applicable to communication systems.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 现有神经网络架构（卷积、循环、Transformer）具有不同的归纳偏置，但ITNet证明它们本质上是可学习积分变换的不同实例。本文提出积分变换网络（ITNet），其核心是一个依赖位置和特征的核函数，由小型MLP实现，能够统一上述架构。ITNet在多个任务上展现灵活性与性能，为通信系统中需要处理序列、局部和全局依赖的问题提供了新颖架构选择。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 现有神经网络架构（卷积、循环、Transformer）具有不同的归纳偏置，但ITNet证明它们本质上是可学习积分变换的不同实例。本文提出积分变换网络（ITNet），其核心是一个依赖位置和特征的核函数，由小型MLP实现，能够统一上述架构。ITNet在多个任务上展现灵活性与性能，为通信系统中需要处理序列、局部和全局依赖的问题提供了新颖架构选择。

quick_skim.title: Uncertainty-Aware Haptic Signal Estimation for Reliable and Resource Efficient Tactile Internet
quick_skim.abstract: The Tactile Internet aims to enable real-time remote haptic interaction; however, the high sampling rates required for transparency in haptic control often lead to severe congestion in multi-user wireless environments. This paper proposes the Agile AI-empowered Haptic (A2HAP) framework, which integrates VarxHAP, a novel probabilistic neural network for joint force and uncertainty estimation, with an error-resilient controller. By employing a hierarchical gating architecture, the system dynamically adapts transmission thresholds to balance model confidence against reliability targets. Simulation results demonstrate that A2HAP suppresses packet rates by up to 45% during peak traffic and reduces resource block consumption by 25% on average. Consequently, the framework supports a 20% increase in user capacity compared to state-of-the-art methods while maintaining the ultra-reliability required for stable teleoperation.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: probabilistic neural network for haptic signal estimation in Tactile Internet
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 用于触觉互联网中触觉信号估计的概率神经网络
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 用于触觉互联网中触觉信号估计的概率神经网络
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 用于触觉互联网中触觉信号估计的概率神经网络
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes A2HAP framework with a probabilistic neural network for joint force and uncertainty estimation, reducing packet rates by 45% in multi-user environments.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对触觉互联网中高采样率导致拥塞的问题，提出A2HAP框架。集成了VarxHAP概率神经网络进行力和不确定性联合估计，并通过分层门控自适应调整传输阈值。仿真表明，在高峰流量下分组速率降低45%，资源块消耗减少25%。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对触觉互联网中高采样率导致拥塞的问题，提出A2HAP框架。集成了VarxHAP概率神经网络进行力和不确定性联合估计，并通过分层门控自适应调整传输阈值。仿真表明，在高峰流量下分组速率降低45%，资源块消耗减少25%。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: deep learning techniques applied to communications

quick_skim.title: RepNN: Tackling spectral bias in deep neural networks via parameter reparameterization
quick_skim.abstract: Deep neural networks (DNNs) have achieved remarkable success in scientific computing, yet they often suffer from spectral bias in capturing oscillatory and multiscale behaviors. In this study, we investigate this limitation by examining the failure of shallow ReLU neural networks in fitting high-frequency functions. This observation identifies two important factors in resolving rapid oscillations: the initial slope scale and the distribution of partition points induced by the networks. Motivated by this analysis, we propose RepNN, a reparameterized neural network model with activation ReLU or tanh designed for high-frequency and multiscale problems. The key idea is to reparameterize the weights and biases in the first hidden layer, which enables effective control of the initial slope scale and provides an appropriate distribution of the initial partition points. Furthermore, treating the reparameterized weights and biases as trainable parameters allows the DNN to achieve adaptive frequency scaling during training. In addition, we derive quantitative estimates for the output and slope magnitudes of the reparameterized DNN to guide the initialization of the proposed method. Numerical experiments, including multiscale one- and four-dimensional function approximations, forward and inverse PDE problems in combination with physics-informed neural networks (PINNs), and operator learning for an earthquake problem using real data, demonstrate that RepNN improves the predicted accuracy of vanilla DNNs in capturing highly oscillatory features with slightly additional computational cost. These results indicate that RepNN provides an effective and flexible approach for overcoming spectral bias and applying DNNs to multiscale problems.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: novel reparameterized neural network to overcome spectral bias
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 新颖的重参数化神经网络，克服频谱偏差
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 新颖的重参数化神经网络，克服频谱偏差
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 新颖的重参数化神经网络，克服频谱偏差
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: RepNN proposes a reparameterized neural network with ReLU/tanh to tackle spectral bias, achieving high-frequency function fitting, relevant for communication channel modeling.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文提出RepNN，一种通过重参数化第一隐藏层权重和偏置来克服频谱偏差的新型神经网络架构。RepNN设计用于高频和多尺度问题，在函数拟合实验中展示了对高频成分的捕捉能力。这种新颖的架构可以作为通信信道建模中处理高频信道响应的有力工具，符合用户寻找新颖神经网络架构的需求。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该论文提出RepNN，一种通过重参数化第一隐藏层权重和偏置来克服频谱偏差的新型神经网络架构。RepNN设计用于高频和多尺度问题，在函数拟合实验中展示了对高频成分的捕捉能力。这种新颖的架构可以作为通信信道建模中处理高频信道响应的有力工具，符合用户寻找新颖神经网络架构的需求。

quick_skim.title: Monotonic Kolmogorov-Arnold Networks: A Theoretical and Empirical Study of Monotonicity as an Inductive Bias
quick_skim.abstract: Monotonicity has been a long-running architectural inductive bias for neural networks, motivated by tabular, scientific, and economic settings where outputs are known to respond monotonically to certain inputs. Existing approaches are MLP- or flow-based and lack per-edge functional transparency; the only Kolmogorov--Arnold Network (KAN) variant with monotonicity, MonoKAN, enforces the constraint only on a restricted parameter subset and requires a projection-style training procedure. We close this gap with \textbf{MKAN}, a KAN with hard monotonicity guaranteed for \emph{all} parameter values via exponential reparameterization of B-spline coefficients, positive edge weights, and a monotone base activation. Training reduces to standard unconstrained gradient descent. Our headline theoretical contribution is a \emph{representation-cost} theorem: any $C^K, K >0$ feature extractor inducing a ball-shaped semantic-neighborhood partition admits a monotone realization of the equivalent neighborhood structure at $N' = N^* + k \le 2N^*$, where $k$ is the number of non-monotone coordinates of the original. The bound is architecture-agnostic and gives a principled sizing rule for monotone encoders. Empirically, MKAN is competitive with state-of-the-art monotone NNs on the SMM/ICML-2024 benchmark while being the only method that combines hard unconstrained monotonicity with KAN's per-edge functional transparency; the $2N^*$ prediction is validated in a self-supervised feature-size sweep on four real datasets, and on a controlled monotone-generative dataset MKAN recovers ground-truth factors with substantially higher Spearman alignment than KAN, MLP, and linear baselines.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: MKAN: a monotonic Kolmogorov-Arnold Network variant
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes MKAN, a Kolmogorov-Arnold network with hard monotonicity guarantees for all parameters via exponential reparameterization and positive weights.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 现有单调KAN方法只对部分参数施加约束且需投影训练。本文提出MKAN，通过B样条系数的指数重参数化、正边权重和单调基激活，实现所有参数下的硬单调性保证。MKAN在多个科学和表格数据集上验证了其单调性和透明度，可推广至需要单调归纳偏置的通信建模等任务。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 现有单调KAN方法只对部分参数施加约束且需投影训练。本文提出MKAN，通过B样条系数的指数重参数化、正边权重和单调基激活，实现所有参数下的硬单调性保证。MKAN在多个科学和表格数据集上验证了其单调性和透明度，可推广至需要单调归纳偏置的通信建模等任务。
